ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Year the movie was released */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the director of the movie */,
  "rating" REAL /* Aggregate rating of the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Duration of the movie in minutes */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language(s) in which the movie is available */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of origin */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the movie */,
  "box_office" REAL /* Box office revenue */,
  "plot_summary" TEXT /* Brief summary of the movie plot */,
  "trailer_url" TEXT /* URL to the movie trailer */,
  "poster_url" TEXT /* URL to the movie poster */,
  "imdb_id" TEXT /* IMDb ID for cross-referencing */,
  "content_rating" TEXT /* Content rating (e.g., PG-13, R) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_director_id FOREIGN KEY ("director_id") REFERENCES directors ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Full name of the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the director */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the director */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the director */,
  "date_of_death" TEXT /* Date of death of the director (if applicable) */,
  "notable_works" TEXT /* List of notable works by the director */,
  "awards" TEXT /* List of awards received by the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who gave the rating */,
  "rating_value" REAL /* Value of the rating */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date when the rating was given */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Short comment by the user when giving the rating */,
  "spoilers" INTEGER /* Indicates if the rating contains spoilers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text content of the review */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was written */,
  "spoilers" INTEGER /* Indicates if the review contains spoilers */,
  "upvotes" INTEGER /* Number of upvotes the review has received */,
  "downvotes" INTEGER /* Number of downvotes the review has received */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., regular user, moderator, admin) */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile picture */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Brief biography of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "subscription_type" TEXT /* Type of subscription (e.g., free, premium) */,
  "watchlist" TEXT /* List of movies the user wants to watch */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie-genre association */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  "primary_genre" INTEGER /* Indicates if the genre is the primary genre for the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_genres_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_genres_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "watch_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each watch history entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who watched the movie */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie watched */,
  "watch_date" TEXT /* Date when the movie was watched */,
  "completion_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has completed watching the movie */,
  "duration_watched" INTEGER /* Duration the user spent watching the movie (in minutes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_watch_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_watch_history_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recommendations" (
  "recommendation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recommendation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being recommended */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recommended movie */,
  "recommendation_date" TEXT /* Date when the recommendation was made */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the recommendation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recommendation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recommendations_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recommendations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who interacted */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie interacted with */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., like, save, share) */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date when the interaction occurred */,
  "interaction_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the user has interacted with the movie in the same way */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interactions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_interactions_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)
